description: Vercel CLI deploy fails with 'website/website' path doubling when run from website/ subdirectory and Root Directory is set to 'website' in Vercel dashboard
workaround: Use deploy hook (curl POST to agents/nel/security/deploy-hook) as primary deploy method
permanent_fix: kai.sh cmd_deploy() uses deploy hook as primary; CLI only as fallback from project root
gate_question: Am I using the deploy hook (not CLI) for Vercel deploys?

description: Mini's nightly runners hold git index.lock during execution, blocking sandbox commits
workaround: Remove lock via queue: kai exec 'rm -f ~/Documents/Projects/Hyo/.git/index.lock'
permanent_fix: Use --no-verify for nightly bulk commits to reduce hook execution time and race conditions
gate_question: Does .git/index.lock exist before attempting a commit from sandbox?
gate_location: Queue exec before any sandbox commit attempt

description: check_aric_day() in agent-growth.sh created trigger marker but never called agent-research.sh, so ARIC cycle appeared triggered but no research happened
permanent_fix: check_aric_day() now checks for both marker AND findings-YYYY-MM-DD.md; only skips if both exist; calls agent-research.sh if findings missing
gate_question: Does findings-YYYY-MM-DD.md exist (not just the trigger marker) before declaring ARIC complete?
gate_location: bin/agent-growth.sh check_aric_day(); morning report ARIC verification

description: Issue ledgers accumulated 128+ false positives over time from known noisy sources (nel scanner noise, API 401s, pip-audit missing, bore.pub tunnel, Aurora legacy logs)
permanent_fix: dex-dedup.py with 5 built-in FP patterns; runs in consolidate.sh Phase 0c daily at 01:00 MT; marks matched entries as resolved_fp with reason
gate_question: Has dex-dedup.py run today? Are new entries being checked against known-fps.json?

description: website/data/feed.json and agents/sam/website/data/feed.json are separate git paths (despite symlink locally); direct writes to one don't propagate to other in git
workaround: Always use publish-to-feed.sh which handles both paths
permanent_fix: Pre-commit hook in .git/hooks/pre-commit enforces dual-path sync; publish-to-feed.sh writes both
gate_question: Did publish-to-feed.sh write to BOTH feed.json paths before pushing?
